channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Terrorists threatened to kill me, church members – Pastor
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A Plateau pastor alleges terrorists threatened to kill him and his church members days after nine of his relatives were killed in an attack.
Read More: https://punchng.com/terrorists-threatened-to-kill-me-church-members-pastor/

item.title: Neymar returns to Santos with questions hanging over his future
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Neymar returns to Santos with his contract ending and questions over his Brazil future after the World Cup. Will he stay or move to MLS?
Read More: https://punchng.com/neymar-returns-to-santos-with-questions-hanging-over-his-future/

A popular Anambra native doctor, Onyeze Jesus, has been sentenced to six years in prison and fined ₦20 million for misleading the public with supernatural

item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Former West Indies captain Sir Garfield "Garry" Sobers has died at the age of 89.

item.title: Historic day at The Open! Who else is in golf's major 62 club?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Lucas Herbert and Sam Burns equalled history with their second round at The 154th Open, becoming the latest players to join an exclusive club with a '62 round' in men's major golf.

item.title: Chidimma Adetshina fights deportation in South African court
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Former Miss Universe Nigeria, Chidimma Adetshina, has appeared before a South African court as she continues to challenge efforts by the country's authorities to deport her over allegations that she is residing in South Africa without legal immigration status. The post Chidimma Adetshina fights depo

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Manchester United midfielder Nicky Butt has advised Kobbie Mainoo to decline participation in England’s World Cup third-place match against France on Saturday. Mainoo concluded the latter half of the previous season as one of Manchester United’s key players under Michael Carrick,

item.title: Tinubu signs executive order to regulate cryptocurrency, virtual assets
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has signed a new executive order establishing a coordinated regulatory framework for virtual assets in Nigeria, with the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN), the Nigeria Revenue Service (NRS) and the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) taking the lead in overseeing the sector. item.title: FG Files Terrorism Charges Against Three Oyo School Kidnappers
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Federal Government has filed a 10-count charge against three men over the abduction of pupils and teachers from a school in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State. The charges, filed before the Federal High Court in Abuja on Friday, accuse the defendants of terrorism, kidnapping, concealment

item.title: Going Into Coaching Is Not My Dream –Ex-Super Eagles Defender

item.title: Cameroon’s phantom gold: Billions slipping through the state’s fingers
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: Illegal gold, porous borders and complicity at the highest levels – Cameroon is struggling to reap the rewards of its mining sector. Gold mining still accounts for barely 1% of national GDP. Part 1 of our investigation.

item.description: The United States military has destroyed a surveillance tower at Iran’s Chah Bahar Shahid Kalantari Port on the Gulf of Oman, which it said the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps had used for decades to track and target commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz. item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Country Director of the United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF), Catherine Russell, has said Nigeria’s certification as free of wild poliovirus in 2020 demonstrated that even difficult public health challenges can be overcome through leadership, community engagement, and partnership.

item.title: Sweetgreen Pares 26% Drop as Parasite Tied to Taco Bell Lettuce
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Sweetgreen Inc. shares surged on Friday, halting a four-day skid, after US regulators said a parasite outbreak that’s sickened thousands across several states was traced to shredded iceberg lettuce served at Taco Bell restaurants.

item.title: From colonial symbol to sanctuary: Paris's Grand Mosque turns 100
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The Grand Mosque of Paris, built in 1926 in the Latin Quarter as a tribute to Muslim soldiers who died for France during World War I, marks its 100th anniversary this year. French historian Benjamin Stora traces its little-known role as a geopolitical bridge – not only between France and Algeria, bu

item.description: A jersey worn by Brazilian football legend Pele during the 1958 FIFA World Cup final has sold for $4.9 million at an auction in New York, making it the second most expensive football shirt ever sold. item.title: Court sentences fake native doctor to 6 years in prison, N20m fine in Anambra
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: An Awka High Court has sentenced one of the arrested fake native doctors, Onyebuchi Okocha aka Onyeze Jesus to six years imprisonment with a fine of N20 million. This is coming as seven suspected fake pastors arrested last month in Anambra were ordered to be remanded in custody until their respectiv

item.title: NBA: Osigwe promises free, fair national election, rejects rigging claims
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: "It is unfortunate that some lawyers and some candidates have chosen to play fast and loose with the truth and allege rigging even when the process has not commenced," he said. The post NBA: Osigwe promises free, fair national election, rejects rigging claims appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: WAFCON 2026: ‘We Want To Repeat History’ — Okoronkwo Reveals Super Falcons’ Mission
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Esther Okoronkwo has declared that the Super Falcons are determined to make history by successfully defending their title at the 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations, Completesports.com reports. Justine Madugu’s side will be aiming to secure a record-extending 11th continental crown in Morocco. Okoron

item.title: Court dismisses Bayelsa traditional ruler’s suit against SPDC
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The traditional ruler alleged that Shell's divestment did not comply with the guidelines stipulated in the Petroleum Industry Act 2021. The post Court dismisses Bayelsa traditional ruler’s suit against SPDC appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: NDLEA, NCC sign MoU in alliance against drug trafficking, piracy
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) and the Nigerian Copyright Commission (NCC) have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) read more NDLEA, NCC sign MoU in alliance against drug trafficking, piracy

item.title: 2026 air cargo rates could rise 15% due to Iran war impacts
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: 2027: Gombe’s Yahaya Inaugurates Reconciliation Committee, Targets One Million Votes For Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Governor Yahaya,added that the state APC must also secure clean sweeps for the governorship seat, all six National Assembly positions, and all 24 House of Assembly seats. The post 2027: Gombe’s Yahaya Inaugurates Reconciliation Committee, Targets One Million Votes For Tinubu appeared first on

item.title: Amazon fixing bug that billed some AWS customers billions of dollars
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Some Amazon customers logged on Friday to a surprise bill estimate claiming that they owed the tech and cloud giant billions in fees.

item.title: Tinubu Signs Executive Order To Coordinate Virtual Assets Regulation
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has signed an executive order establishing a coordinated regulatory framework for virtual assets to tackle fraud, protect investors and close gaps exploited by unregistered operators. The Presidential Executive Order on Virtual Assets Coordination, 2026, also creates a Virtual

item.title: Tinubu signs executive order on virtual assets, establishes council to harmonise crypto regulation
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu signs executive order establishing a Virtual Asset Council to harmonise crypto regulation in Nigeria, closing gaps and protecting citizens
Read More: https://punchng.com/tinubu-signs-executive-order-on-virtual-assets-establishes-council-to-harmonise-crypto-regulation/

item.title: U.S ends indefinite stay for foreign students, journalists
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: … Sets 4-year limit on visas By Nkiruka nnorom The United States has announced a new rule ending indefinite stay for foreign students, exchange visitors and journalists, and capped their period of admission at a maximum of four years. The final rule, announced Thursday by the Department of Homeland

item.title: Nigeria Customs releases N7.61 billion retirement benefits to 4,237 retirees
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Nigeria Customs Service has disbursed N7.61 billion in retirement benefits to 4,237 retirees through nine Pension Fund Administrators. The post Nigeria Customs releases N7.61 billion retirement benefits to 4,237 retirees appeared first on Nairametrics.
